goals: Expand the effect library with 5 lens-based GLSL distortion nodes
Each node plugs into the existing `CoolVideoGenerator` multi-effect chain without any changes to downstream nodes
Artists can preview distortion in real time inside the node widget before rendering

userStories.title: Fisheye Effect Node
userStories.description: As a ComfyUI artist, I want a Fisheye node that applies barrel distortion to an image so that I can simulate an ultra-wide / fisheye lens look. Inputs: - `strength` (FLOAT, default 0.5, min 0.0, max 1.0, step 0.01) â€” distortion intensity - `zoom` (FLOAT, default 1.0, min 0.5, max 2.0, step 0.01) â€” compensating zoom to avoid black borders

acceptanceCriteria.text: Node `CoolFisheyeEffect` appears in ComfyUI under the correct category
GLSL fragment shader `shaders/glsl/fisheye.frag` implements barrel distortion via polar-coordinate UV remapping
Live WebGL2 preview inside the node widget updates when `strength` or `zoom` sliders change
Effect is visually verified through `CoolVideoGenerator` â€” rendered video frames show barrel distortion

userStories.title: Pincushion Effect Node
userStories.description: As a ComfyUI artist, I want a Pincushion node that applies pincushion (inverse barrel) distortion so that I can simulate the compression warp of a telephoto lens. Inputs: - `strength` (FLOAT, default 0.5, min 0.0, max 1.0, step 0.01) â€” distortion intensity - `zoom` (FLOAT, default 1.0, min 0.5, max 2.0, step 0.01) â€” compensating zoom

acceptanceCriteria.text: GLSL fragment shader `shaders/glsl/pincushion.frag` implements inverse-barrel distortion (edges pulled inward)
Live WebGL2 preview updates in real time

userStories.title: Chromatic Aberration Effect Node
userStories.description: As a ComfyUI artist, I want a Chromatic Aberration node that separates the RGB channels radially so that I can simulate lens colour dispersion (fringing at edges). Inputs: - `strength` (FLOAT, default 0.01, min 0.0, max 0.1, step 0.001) â€” magnitude of channel separation - `radial` (BOOLEAN, default True) â€” when True, separation grows toward edges (radial); when False, uniform lateral shift

acceptanceCriteria.text: GLSL fragment shader `shaders/glsl/chromatic_aberration.frag` samples R, G, B channels at slightly offset UVs
Radial mode increases separation toward image corners; lateral mode applies uniform constant offset
Live WebGL2 preview updates in real time

userStories.title: Vignette Effect Node
userStories.description: As a ComfyUI artist, I want a Vignette node that darkens image edges so that I can add cinematic depth or draw attention to the centre. Inputs: - `strength` (FLOAT, default 0.5, min 0.0, max 1.0, step 0.01) â€” darkness at edges - `radius` (FLOAT, default 0.75, min 0.1, max 1.5, step 0.01) â€” how far from centre the vignette begins - `softness` (FLOAT, default 0.5, min 0.0, max 1.0, step 0.01) â€” falloff smoothness

acceptanceCriteria.text: GLSL fragment shader `shaders/glsl/vignette.frag` computes radial distance from centre and applies smooth darkening
Live WebGL2 preview updates in real time

userStories.title: Tilt-Shift Effect Node
userStories.description: As a ComfyUI artist, I want a Tilt-Shift node that blurs the top and bottom bands of the image while keeping the centre sharp so that I can create a miniature/model-world illusion. Inputs: - `focus_center` (FLOAT, default 0.5, min 0.0, max 1.0, step 0.01) â€” vertical position of the sharp band (0 = top, 1 = bottom) - `focus_width` (FLOAT, default 0.2, min 0.0, max 1.0, step 0.01) â€” fraction of image height kept in focus - `blur_strength` (FLOAT, default 0.5, min 0.0, max 1.0, step 0.01) â€” intensity of blur in out-of-focus bands - `angle` (FLOAT, optional, default 0.0, min 0.0, max 360.0, step 1.0) â€” rotation of the focus band in degrees; reserved for future use, defaults to horizontal band (0Â°)

acceptanceCriteria.text: GLSL fragment shader `shaders/glsl/tilt_shift.frag` applies a blur kernel (Gaussian approximation) that scales with distance from the focus band
Live WebGL2 preview updates in real time when any slider changes
Effect is visually verified through `CoolVideoGenerator` â€” rendered video shows sharp centre band with blurred top/bottom

functionalRequirements.description: Each new node must follow the file layout convention: `nodes/<effect>_effect.py` (Python) + `shaders/glsl/<effect>.frag` (GLSL) + `web/<effect>_effect.js` (JS frontend)
Each GLSL shader must accept the standard uniform contract: `uniform sampler2D u_image`, `uniform float u_time`, `uniform vec2 u_resolution`, plus effect-specific uniforms
Each Python node must call `build_effect_params()` from `nodes/effect_params.py` to construct its output
Each JS frontend extension must use `mount_effect_node_widget` from `web/effect_node_widget.js` to attach the WebGL2 preview canvas
All five nodes must be registered in `NODE_CLASS_MAPPINGS` and `NODE_DISPLAY_NAME_MAPPINGS` in `__init__.py`
GL resource cleanup in the Python backend must follow the established `try/finally` teardown order (vao â†’ vbo â†’ fbo â†’ renderbuffer â†’ texture â†’ program â†’ ctx)
No new Python package dependencies may be introduced
